title: 5 Processing a Receiver General Deposit
body_text:
5	Processing a Receiver General Deposit
5.1	Overview of Processing a Receiver General Deposit
Your financial institution processes deposits made by Federal departments and agencies (e.g., prisons, RCMP) on behalf of the Receiver General of Canada (Receiver General) to the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account held at your financial institution.
Report the total value of cash and/or cheques received using the Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow. You must submit the Receiver General Deposit Request the same day you receive a deposit.
Central 1 uses the information to sweep the Receiver General Concentrator holding accounts held at your financial institution and aggregates the deposits for credit to the Receiver General. Specifically, Central 1 issues a pre-authorized debit (PAD) against each Receiver General Concentrator holding account and credits the funds to the Receiver General Concentrator account held at Central 1. Central 1 sends the Receiver General a wire transfer and a summary of deposits by Routing Authorization Number.
5.2	Verifying and Processing a Canadian Dollar Receiver General Deposit
Verify and process Canadian dollar Receiver General deposits as follows:
Verify the total value of Canadian dollar cash and the total value of Canadian cheques to the totals on the Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334. See Section 4.1, Preparing Receiver General Deposits for procedures on completing the form.
Ensure that all cheques are acceptable for deposit (e.g., verify the date, itemГўВҖВҷs MICR, signature). See the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
Verify that all cheques are endorsed in the upper right quadrant of the back of each item. The endorsement must include the following text in black ink: ГўВҖВңFor deposit only to the credit of the Receiver General on behalf of [Department acronym] Authorization Number (e.g., 12345). If dishonoured, return to 809-71620.ГўВҖВқ See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Example Receiver General Deposit Item Endorsement
Credit the total dollar value of the Canadian cash and/or cheques to the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account. Follow your internal policies and procedures. The total amount on the Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334, should always correspond to the total amount credited to the Receiver General Concentrator holding account. If multiple deposit slips are received from a Receiver General agent, post the deposit slip totals to the account separately.
Initial the deposit slip and provide a copy to the depositor.
Prepare and process the Canadian dollar cheques as a separate Canadian dollar deposit in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on creating deposits in Branch Capture. Ensure that you complete the following:
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңReceiver GeneralГўВҖВқ in a Deposit Reference field when creating the deposit in Branch Capture.
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the Central 1 Receiver General Concentrator account transit number (809-71620) in the Deposit Transit and Deposit Route fields for each Receiver General deposit item in Branch Capture. This is important because dishonoured Receiver General deposit items must be returned to Central 1 for processing. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
Note:	Failure to enter the correct return transit will result in the items being returned to your financial institution. See Section 6.3 Managing Chargebacks for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account for more information.
Retain copies of the following documents for your records per your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policy: Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334, and itemized tape listings of cheque bundles.
Submit a Receiver General Deposit Request in Service Now. See Section 5.4, Submitting a Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow for procedures.
5.3	Verifying and Processing a US Dollar Receiver General Deposit
Verify and process US dollar Receiver General deposits as follows:
Ensure that the cheques are acceptable for deposit (e.g., verify the date, itemГўВҖВҷs MICR, signature). See the Deposit Processing Part for more information.
Ensure that all cheques are endorsed in the upper right quadrant of the back of each item. The endorsement must include the following text in black ink: ГўВҖВңFor deposit only to the credit of the RG on behalf of [Department acronym] Authorization Number (e.g., 12345). If dishonoured, return to 71620-809.ГўВҖВқ See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Example Receiver General Deposit Item Endorsement
Convert the total value of all US cash and cheques to Canadian dollars using the daily posted exchange rates. Retain a copy for your records.
Record the Canadian dollar equivalent value of all US dollar cash and cheques on a Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334. See Section 4.1, Preparing Receiver General Deposits, for a description of the fields. Provide a receipt to the depositor.
Credit the Canadian dollar equivalent value of the US cash and/or cheques to the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account. Follow your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs internal policies and procedures. The total amount on the Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334, should always correspond to the total amount credited to the Receiver General Concentrator holding account. If multiple deposit slips are received from a Receiver General agent, post the deposit slip totals to the account separately.
Prepare and process the US dollar cheques as a separate US dollar deposit in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on processing items in Branch Capture. Ensure that you complete the following:
ГўВҲВ’
Record ГўВҖВңReceiver GeneralГўВҖВқ in the Deposit Reference field when creating the deposit in Branch Capture.
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the Central 1 Receiver General Concentrator account transit number (809-71620) in the Deposit Transit and Deposit Route fields for each Receiver General deposit item in Branch Capture. This is important because dishonoured Receiver General deposit items must be returned to Central 1 for processing. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
Retain copies of the following documents for your records per your financial institutionГўВҖВҷs policy: Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334, US dollar currency conversion slips, and itemized tape listings of cheque bundles.
Submit a Receiver General Deposit Request in Service Now. See Section 5.4, Submitting a Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow for procedures.
5.4	Submitting a Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow
Use ServiceNow to submit a Receiver General deposit request to Central 1 on the date you receive the deposit. Failure to do so will prevent Central 1 from issuing a PAD to debit the Receiver General holding account and from crediting the Receiver General.
Note:	If you donГўВҖВҷt have access to ServiceNow, contact your User Management Security Officer to request access.
Submit a Receiver General deposit request on ServiceNow as follows:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 3: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Accessing the Service Catalogue
Select
Receiver General Deposit Request
in the Paper Payments category. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Selecting ГўВҖВңReceiver General Deposit RequestГўВҖВқ
Complete the Receiver General Deposit Request as follows. See Figure 7. Your financial institution representativeГўВҖВҷs name pre-populates.
Important
:	If you are processing more than one deposit from the agent, you must enter each deposit separately. You can enter multiple deposits in one Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow by adding additional rows as described.
ГўВ‘
Enter the appropriate transit number.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Enter the account number for the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account.
ГўВ‘Вў
Enter the authorization number of the applicable Receiver General department.
Important
:	Ensure that the account number and Receiver General authorization number are correct, or the deposit will be rejected.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
Enter the deposit amount.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Select the plus sign (
+
) to add a new row and include an additional deposit.
Note: 	Select the  ГўВҖВң
ГўВЁВҜ
ГўВҖВқ to delete a row.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select
Submit
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created. Alternatively, select the trash can to delete all data and start over.
Figure 7: Receiver General Deposit Request Form in ServiceNow
Record the ServiceNow ticket number on Receiver General Account Deposit Slip, Form 6334.
END
